paper.title: Counterfactual Load Forecasting with LLM-Structured Events and Representation Learning
paper.subtitle: News-Aware Counterfactual Load Analysis Framework for estimating news-related demand perturbations under observational confounding

No_News.label: Reference treatment
No_News.description: The same historical operating context evaluated with the no-news reference treatment representation.

Env_Thermal.label: Thermal event
Env_Thermal.description: Heat and temperature stress signals that often alter cooling demand.

Env_Disaster.label: Natural disaster
Env_Disaster.description: Bushfires, storms, floods, and infrastructure-disruption weather events.

Soc_Calendar.label: Calendar event
Soc_Calendar.description: Holidays, public events, and scheduled activity changes.

Soc_Economic.label: Socio-economic event
Soc_Economic.description: Business activity, policy, restriction, and demand-response signals.

Grid_Reliability.label: Grid reliability
Grid_Reliability.description: Reserve scarcity, outages, load shedding, and system warnings.

events.text: Inaugural Rugby League World Cup Nines at Bankwest Stadium (Sydney) drew crowds of ~12,528 (Fri) and ~15,684 (Sat), creating concentrated event-driven electricity demand at the stadium and nearby precinct.
events.justification: Large scheduled sporting event in Sydney increases localized electricity consumption (stadium lighting, HVAC, broadcasting, transport/retail activity) and is a deterministic calendar driver.

events.text: The Everest horse race at Royal Randwick (Randwick, NSW) attracted a reported crowd of 40,912 on Everest Day, representing a major spring racing event that increases local electricity demand for lighting, HVAC, catering and broadcast operations.
events.justification: The article reports a large, completed sporting event in Sydney (Randwick) with tens of thousands attending — a deterministic calendar event that raises localized demand from venue operations, hospitality and media coverage.

summary.label: Large generator outage with demand response

events.text: A strong cool change is forecast to arrive across New South Wales tomorrow after several hot days.
events.justification: Weather event scenario for counterfactual inference.

events.text: Several large manufacturing sites in New South Wales are restarting full production tomorrow after maintenance outages.
events.justification: Industrial activity scenario for counterfactual inference.

events.text: Most offices, schools, and retail businesses across New South Wales will close tomorrow for a public holiday.
events.justification: Calendar event scenario for counterfactual inference.

events.text: New South Wales will enter stay-at-home restrictions tomorrow, with many offices and non-essential venues closed and residents spending more time at home.
events.justification: Mobility restriction scenario for counterfactual inference.

events.text: Severe storms are expected to damage parts of the distribution network in New South Wales tomorrow, with outages possible in several suburbs.
events.justification: Grid reliability scenario for counterfactual inference.

customScenarios.label: Large generator outage with demand response

events.text: A large coal-fired generating unit in New South Wales has unexpectedly tripped, tightening supply and triggering demand-response alerts for large customers.
events.justification: Supply-demand response scenario for counterfactual inference.

notebook.description: Exported from the tutorial notebook first test batch: observed-news prediction, reference-treatment counterfactual prediction, and custom-news scenario predictions under the same historical context.
